reasoning: The known reference value for the past 360 days in ACLED is exactly 0.000. Despite news reports of actual protests in the Dominican Republic during 2025, ACLED appears to not be recording them (either due to lag, gaps in coverage for the country, or definitional reasons). If this reporting gap continues, the 30-day windows will also record 0 protests, resulting in 0 > 0 (resolves NO). Even if ACLED back-fills the data to accurately reflect the past year, statistical principles dictate that count variables like protest occurrences often have a right-skewed distribution. Thus, any randomly selected 30-day window has a less than 50% chance of exceeding the 360-day average. Given both the likely data reporting issue and the mathematical base rate, the probability of resolving YES is quite low. I have increased the probabilities toward 0.5 for the later dates to account for the possibility of ACLED fixing its reporting stream or an unforeseen spike in political unrest.
